scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured tool outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within laboratory and research operations scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured tool outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: Skipped files reporting added to Output Requirements; encoding detection reporting added; pre-flight scope gate added; all v1 gaps closed

reliability.note: Fallback template with detailed notes; stress-case checklist; pre-flight gate prevents partial content suggestion before refusal

performance_context.note: SKILL.md is 193 lines — slightly long but well-organized; no references/ folder

agent_usability.note: Skipped files count and list now in Output Requirements; encoding detection reporting added; pre-flight gate in Workflow Step 1

human_usability.note: Description is discoverable; 'garbled text' trigger language natural; AI tool mention in description is helpful

security.note: No credentials; local file I/O; encrypted PDF handling documented; no mention of malicious PDF content risks

maintainability.note: Clean script separation; requirements.txt with version pins; repair levels modular; all fixes applied cleanly

agent_specific.note: Pre-flight gate closes partial content suggestion gap; escape hatches strong; composability moderate via JSON export

key_strengths: Skipped files reporting (count + filenames) now mandatory in batch mode Output Requirements, closing the primary v1 transparency gap
Pre-flight scope gate in Workflow Step 1 prevents any partial content suggestion before refusal
Encoding detection reporting now mandatory in Assumptions, improving output traceability
Three repair levels (minimal/standard/aggressive) with clear trade-off documentation enable appropriate tool selection
JSON export mode for AI tool import is a unique feature extending utility beyond simple file repair

recommendations.title: Refusal message wording may deviate from template in edge cases
recommendations.problem: The scope refusal for content editing requests occasionally shows minor wording variation from the documented template.
recommendations.root_cause: Input Validation section does not mark the refusal message as verbatim-required.
recommendations.fix: Add 'respond verbatim:' before the refusal message template in Input Validation to enforce exact wording.

recommendations.title: No mention of malicious PDF content risks
recommendations.problem: PDFs can contain embedded JavaScript or malicious content that could be triggered during processing, but the skill does not warn about this risk.
recommendations.root_cause: Security section focuses on credential safety and file I/O but does not address malicious file content.
recommendations.fix: Add to Limitations: 'PDFs may contain embedded scripts or malicious content. Process only files from trusted sources. Consider using a sandboxed environment for untrusted PDFs.'
